data.title: Gold Royalty misses Q4 top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 and beyond outlook
data.description:
• Gold Royalty press release (NYSE:GROY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $3.36M (+229.4% Y/Y) misses by $1.64M.
  • 1,445 GEOs for the quarter.
  • Positive operating cash flow of $1.3 million and Adjusted EBITDA of $1.2 million.

  • 2025 and Five-Year Outlook

    • "Total GEOs are currently expected to increase to 5,700-7,000 in 2025, a year when three of our seven cash flowing assets continue to ramp up towards full production. This outlook represents an increase of approximately 16% from 2024.
    • Peer-leading growth of over 360% from 2024 levels is expected in the next five years, with GEOs forecasted to increase to between 23,000 and 28,000 GEOs in 2029. The projected five-year outlook reflects continued contributions from our cornerstone producing assets, as well as new production from assets currently in development. See the below "2025 Outlook" and "Five-Year Outlook" sections.
    • Going forward, the Company's balance sheet is expected to be strengthened by greater expected cash flow generation, stable and low operating costs and our recently improved credit terms. While the Company's capital allocation priorities continue to focus on debt repayment and disciplined growth, the increased facility terms provide flexibility to pursue opportunities as they may arise."

data.title: PDD Holdings Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.76 beats by $0.04, revenue of $15.15B misses by $860M
data.description:
• PDD Holdings press release (NASDAQ:PDD): Q4 Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.76 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $15.15B (+24.0% Y/Y) misses by $860M.
  • Revenues from online marketing services and others were RMB57,011.1 million (US$7,810.5 million), an increase of 17% from RMB48,675.6 million in the same quarter of 2023.
  • Revenues from transaction services were RMB53,599.0 million (US$7,343.0 million), an increase of 33% from RMB40,205.4 million in the same quarter of 2023.

data.title: H World Group reports Q4 results
data.description:
• H World Group press release (NASDAQ:HTHT): Q4 revenue increased 7.8% year-over-year to RMB6.0 billion (US$825 million) in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • GAAP EPADS of $0.02.
  • A total of 11,147 hotels or 1,088,218 hotel rooms in operation as of December 31, 2024.
  • Hotel turnover increased 16.5% year-over-year to RMB23.7 billion in the fourth quarter of 2024 and increased 15.5% year-over-year for the full year of 2024.
  • For the first quarter of 2025, H World expects its revenue growth to be in the range of 0%-4%, compared to the first quarter of 2024 or in the range of 3%-7% excluding DH. H World expects its manachised and franchised revenue growth to be in the range of 18%-22%, compared to the first quarter of 2024.
  • For the full year of 2025, H World expects revenue growth to be in the range of 2%-6%, compared to the full year of 2024, or in the range of 5%-9% excluding DH. H World expects its manachised and franchised revenue growth to be in the range of 17%-21%, compared to the full year of 2024.
  • For the full year of 2025, H World expects to open around 2,300 hotels and close around 600 hotels.

data.title: Yiren Digital reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Yiren Digital press release (NYSE:YRD): Q4 total net revenue was RMB1,452.2 million (US$199.0 million), representing an increase of 14.0% from RMB1,274.3 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Basic and diluted income per ADS were RMB3.8 (US$0.5) and RMB3.8 (US$0.5) respectively, compared to a basic income per ADS of RMB6.5 and a diluted income per ADS of RMB6.5 in the same period of 2023.

data.title: Shoe Carnival Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 beats by $0.10, revenue of $262.9M misses by $13.02M
data.description:
• Shoe Carnival press release (NASDAQ:SCVL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $262.9M (-6.2% Y/Y) misses by $13.02M.
  • Fiscal 2025 Outlook
  • Net Sales: $1.15 billion to $1.23 billion, representing a range of down 4 percent to up 2 percent versus Fiscal 2024.
    • GAAP EPS: $1.60 to $2.10, inclusive of the rebanner strategy’s initial year costs.
    • Capital Expenditures: $45 to $60 million.

data.title: QXO to buy Beacon Roofing Supply for $124.35/share
data.description: QXO (NYSE:QXO) +12.2% and Beacon Roofing Supply (NASDAQ:BECN) +2% pre-market Thursday after the roofing and building products distributors announced a merger agreement in which QXO will acquire Beacon for $124.35/share in cash, valuing Beacon at ~$7.7B, or $11B including debt.

data.title: FactSet Research Systems Non-GAAP EPS of $4.28 beats by $0.10, revenue of $570.7M in-line
data.description:
• FactSet Research Systems press release (NYSE:FDS): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.28 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $570.7M (+4.5% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Organic Q2 ASV of $2,276.2 million, up 4.1% year over year.
  • Fiscal 2025 guidance updated. Expected organic ASV growth of $100 million to $130 million (approximately 4.4% to 5.8%), GAAP revenues in the range of $2,305 million to $2,325 million, adjusted operating margin in the range of 36% to 37%, and adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $16.80 to $17.40.

data.title: Nvidia to invest hundreds of billions in US made chips, electronics - report
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) plans to spend hundreds of billion dollars on chips and other electronics made in the U.S. over the next four years, the Financial Times reported.

“Overall, we will procure, over the course of the next four years, probably half a trillion dollars worth of electronics in total,” Jensen Huang, Nvidia’s co-founder and CEO told the news outlet.

“And I think we can easily see ourselves manufacturing several hundred billion of it here in the US,” Huang added.

Nvidia did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Huang noted that Nvidia's latest Blackwell systems are now being made in the U.S. and added that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) investing in the U.S. provides for a substantial step up in the company's supply chain resilience.

Huang also said that If any disaster were to threaten manufacturing in Taiwan, he said, “it will be uncomfortable but it should be ok,” according to the report.

The Nvidia chief also talked about Chinese tech giant Huawei Technologies, which has seen a resurgence not in its smartphone and AI chip development business.

"Huawei is the single most formidable technology company in China,” said Huang. He noted that U.S.-led efforts to restrict Huawei have been “done poorly” given Huawei’s continued success.

“I think that their presence in AI is growing every single year,” said Huang. “We can’t assume that they are not going to be a factor.”

Last year, it was reported that Chinese internet companies and telecommunications operators were testing Huawei's new chip known as Ascend 910C. Huawei also left behind Apple in the Chinese smartphone market share in the fourth quarter of 2024, according to data from Counterpoint Research. 

In addition, Huang denied reports that Nvidia was involved in talks to form a consortium with companies such as TSM to invest in Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) and stopped short of pledging to use its U.S. chip making services as part of that onshoring, the report added.

"We evaluate their foundry technology on a regular basis, and we are ongoing in doing that,” Huang noted, adding that Nvidia was also looking at Intel’s chip packaging services. “We look for opportunities to be a customer of theirs,” the report noted.

Nvidia has been hosting its annual GTC event this week, which has drawn a lot of attention from the world over, in part due to AI advancements.

data.title: Stock index futures muted as Fed sticks to its rate cut outlook for the year
data.description:
Stock index futures were steady on Thursday, as the Federal Reserve kept its outlook of two rate cuts in the year intact.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were unchanegd, and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.23%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.98%.

U.S. stocks pulled back from their session highs on Wednesday, but still ended solidly higher after Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell suggested that inflation caused by tariffs would only be "transitory."

"Tariffs will raise inflation and then lower growth, so the pattern is not unexpected, and of course there is considerable uncertainty given erratic government policy," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

As widely expected, the Fed's policymaking arm kept its policy interest rate unchanged at 4.25%–4.50% on Wednesday, even as the U.S. economic outlook has grown murkier.

"Our U.S. economists think that while the Fed may be anticipating a slowdown in the hard data, they are on hold until that evidence emerges," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

A clear majority of the FOMC now see risks tilted towards higher inflation but towards weaker growth and higher unemployment, Reid added. 

President Trump, however, said the Fed should cut interest rates as tariffs start to "ease" into the economy, after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said tariffs may delay further progress in bringing inflation to the 2% target. 

"The Fed is slowing the reduction of its balance sheet. This reflects the fact that liquidity demand is not slowing so aggressively, and should necessarily be seen as an easing," Donovan said.

On the economic calendar for the day, the initial jobless claims report will land before the opening bell, and is expected to rise to 224K.

The March Philadelphia Fed manufacturing index will also land at the same time, and is forecasted to come in at 8.8.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Tesla recalls thousands of Cybertrucks over exterior panel issue
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is recalling 46,096 Cybertrucks in the U.S. over an exterior panel that can detach while driving.

The recall population includes all model year 2024 and 2025 Cybertruck vehicles produced from Nov. 13, 2023, to Feb. 27, 2025.

The Cybertruck is equipped with a cosmetic applique along the exterior of the vehicle, known as the cant rail, which is an assembly comprised of an electrocoated steel stamping joined to a stainless-steel panel with structural adhesive.

This stainless-steel exterior trim panel can delaminate and detach from the vehicle, increasing the risk of a crash, the recall report read.

Tesla service will replace the cant rail assembly, free of charge.

The Cybertruck has been subject to multiple recalls to date. In October 2024, around 27,000 Cybertrucks were recalled because of a software glitch causing delays in the rearview camera display.

Earlier, in April 2024, nearly 4,000 units faced recalls over accelerator pedal concerns that could result in unintended acceleration. These incidents highlight ongoing challenges in the Cybertruck's rollout.

The electric pickup truck concept was first announced by Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) in 2019, but pilot production could only begin in 2023 amid battery-supply constraints and manufacturing issues.

Shares of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) were down 0.77% in premarket trade on Thursday and are down nearly 42% YTD.

More on Tesla
